takeaway: NVIDIA is the dominant AI computing platform with an extraordinary growth trajectory. The CUDA software ecosystem creates a deep competitive moat, and data center revenue is growing at triple-digit rates driven by AI training and inference demand.

assessment.business_quality: Exceptional. Gross margins of ~75% and net margins of ~55% reflect a premium-priced, software-like business model in hardware. ROIC of ~85% is extraordinary and demonstrates the economic power of the CUDA platform monopoly.
assessment.moat: Extremely wide moat. The CUDA software ecosystem is the de facto standard for GPU computing, creating massive switching costs for developers. This software lock-in reinforces hardware demand, forming a powerful flywheel that competitors cannot easily replicate.
assessment.pricing_power: Extraordinary pricing power. NVIDIA commands premium pricing for its data center GPUs due to unmatched performance and the CUDA ecosystem lock-in. Customers have limited alternatives at the scale required for AI training.
assessment.capital_allocation: Management under Jensen Huang has demonstrated exceptional strategic vision. Capital is reinvested aggressively in R&D to maintain technological leadership. The low dividend yield reflects growth reinvestment priorities.
assessment.red_flags: Key risks include cyclical demand for gaming GPUs, geopolitical restrictions on chip exports to China, potential competition from custom AI chips (ASICs), and extremely elevated valuation multiples. The P/E of 65x leaves little room for execution missteps.
missing_evidence: No real-time data center revenue breakdown
